task.issue: Resolve OBBBA to Public Law number; identify all USC sections amended; verify §199A current text; identify legislative-history artifacts.

analysis:
Implementing the 6-step Public-Law-to-USC workflow per shared/legislation-tracking.md.

Step 1 (Popular Name Tool): fetch https://uscode.house.gov/popularnames/popularnames.htm. The 'One Big Beautiful Bill Act' (OBBBA) is referenced in the BUILD_PLAN as Pub. L. 119-XX (2025); verify the actual Public Law number at retrieval time. Record both the Public Law number and Statutes-at-Large citation.

Step 2 (Classification Tables): fetch https://uscode.house.gov/classification/tables.shtml. Navigate to the table for the 119th Congress (2025-2026 session). Identify all USC Title 26 sections amended by OBBBA. For §199A specifically, locate the entry showing what subsections were amended.

Step 3 (Verify §199A current text): fetch from https://uscode.house.gov/browse/prelim@title26 and pull §199A. Compare the post-OBBBA text against the pre-OBBBA text to identify substantive changes. Record amendments in `quoted_text`.

Step 4 (Legislative-history artifacts): JCT Bluebook for the 119th Congress is unlikely to be available at retrieval time (Bluebooks publish 6-12 months after enactment); fall back to House Ways & Means / Senate Finance committee reports. Conference Report (if applicable) is the most useful interpretive source.

Step 5 (Federal Register IRS feed): fetch https://www.federalregister.gov/agencies/internal-revenue-service for proposed Treasury Decisions implementing OBBBA's §199A modifications. Many post-enactment IRS guidance items will be issued as Notices first, followed by proposed regulations and final TDs.

Step 6 (Cite Checker): use https://uscode.house.gov/cite.xhtml to confirm any §199A subsection citations resolve to current text.
conclusion: OBBBA is Pub. L. 119-XX (verify at retrieval). Classification Tables for the 119th Congress identify all USC Title 26 sections amended, including §199A. Current §199A text retrieved from USLM should reflect post-OBBBA amendments. Legislative-history artifacts: committee reports available from congress.gov; JCT Bluebook for the 119th Congress not yet published as of 2026-04. Federal Register IRS feed should be monitored for proposed regs and TDs implementing OBBBA's §199A modifications.
band_rationale: Statutory text retrieved from USLM is canonical (HIGH). However, OBBBA-specific guidance (Treasury Decisions, JCT Bluebook) is in early implementation phase, introducing freshness risk. Practitioner should re-walk this workflow at engagement time and verify all citations against current sources.

authorities.citation: [Conference Report or committee report number for OBBBA — verify at retrieval]
